project: MVP2 Part 1 — Foundation: Data Access, Schema Migration & Config
description: Foundational data access layer, schema migration infrastructure, and config tuning for MVP2. Adds read/write methods to StatsStore and EdgeStore, establishes schema migration registry, and tunes note_type_priors defaults. Pure internal API additions — no new MCP tools.

userStories.title: Add salience and penalty update methods to StatsStore
userStories.description: As a developer implementing reinforcement, I need methods to read node stats and update salience/penalty counters atomically so that reinforcement logic doesn't require raw SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsStore.get_node_stats(node_id: str) -> dict | None returns a dict with all node_stats columns for a given node, or None if not found
StatsStore.update_salience(node_id: str, delta: float) -> None atomically adjusts salience by delta, clamping to [0.0, 1.0]; creates the row (salience=0.5+delta) if absent
StatsStore.increment_ignored(node_id: str) -> None atomically increments ignored_count; creates row if absent
StatsStore.increment_cited(node_id: str) -> None atomically increments cited_count; creates row if absent
StatsStore.increment_misleading(node_id: str) -> None atomically increments misleading_count; creates row if absent
StatsStore.update_spaced_rep_strength(node_id: str, delta: float) -> None atomically adjusts spaced_rep_strength, clamping to [0.0, 1.0]
Schema change: add cited_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 column to node_stats table via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with fallback for existing databases
All methods use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE pattern consistent with existing increment_node_stats
Unit tests verify each method (insert-on-absent and update-on-existing paths)
make check passes

userStories.title: Add working memory read and eviction methods to StatsStore
userStories.description: As a developer implementing lithos-enrich consolidation and WM eviction, I need methods to query and evict working memory rows.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsStore.get_working_memory(task_id: str) -> list[dict] returns all working_memory rows for a task, ordered by activation_count descending

StatsStore.evict_working_memory(*, completed_task_ids: list[str], ttl_days: int) -> int deletes rows where task_id is in completed_task_ids OR last_seen_at is older than ttl_days ago; returns count of rows deleted
Unit tests cover both methods, including edge cases (empty task, no expired rows)
make check passes

userStories.title: Add enrich_queue read/write methods to StatsStore
userStories.description: As a developer implementing lithos-enrich, I need methods to enqueue enrichment work and drain pending entries.
acceptanceCriteria: StatsStore.enqueue(trigger_type: str, *, node_id: str | None = None, task_id: str | None = None) -> None inserts a row into enrich_queue; exactly one of node_id or task_id must be provided, raises ValueError otherwise

StatsStore.drain_pending_nodes() -> list[dict] atomically claims unprocessed rows where node_id IS NOT NULL AND processed_at IS NULL, returns [{node_id, trigger_types, claimed_ids}] deduplicated by node_id preserving all distinct trigger types per node. Uses SELECT+UPDATE within one transaction (no UPDATE RETURNING). Precedence: note.created and note.updated are never discarded when other triggers are present

StatsStore.drain_pending_tasks() -> list[dict] atomically claims unprocessed task rows where node_id IS NULL AND processed_at IS NULL, returns [{task_id, claimed_ids}] using same atomic SELECT+UPDATE pattern
No separate mark_processed() method — drain methods atomically mark rows as processed at claim time
StatsStore.requeue_failed(claimed_ids: list[int]) -> int resets processed_at=NULL on given row IDs, returns count of rows reset
Unit test: enqueue(trigger_type, node_id=None, task_id=None) raises ValueError
Unit test: enqueue(trigger_type, node_id='x', task_id='y') raises ValueError
Unit tests cover enqueue and atomic drain lifecycle (rows enqueued after drain are not in claimed set)
Unit test: requeue_failed resets processed_at and rows reappear in next drain
make check passes

userStories.title: Add edge weight adjustment method to EdgeStore
userStories.description: As a developer implementing reinforcement (strengthening/weakening edges), I need an atomic weight adjustment method so that concurrent updates don't clobber each other.
acceptanceCriteria: EdgeStore.adjust_weight(edge_id: str, delta: float) -> float | None atomically adjusts weight by delta, clamping to [0.0, 1.0], returns new weight; returns None if edge not found
EdgeStore.list_edges_between(node_ids: list[str], *, edge_type: str | None = None, namespace: str | None = None) -> list[dict] returns all edges where both from_id and to_id are in node_ids
Unit tests verify clamping, absent edge, and the between-query
make check passes

userStories.title: Schema migration registry and runner
userStories.description: As the system, I need a migration registry that tracks which schema versions have been applied to notes, and an idempotent runner that can evolve note schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: Registry location: config.storage.lithos_store_path / 'migrations' / 'registry.json' — relative to configured storage root, not hardcoded data/ path
Registry structure: {current_version: int, applied: [{from: int, to: int, name: str, applied_at: str}]}
New module src/lithos/lcma/migrations.py with MigrationRegistry class accepting registry_path: Path and run_migrations(knowledge, registry) function
Each migration is a named function registered in a migration list
Migrations are idempotent: running an already-applied migration is a no-op
Migrations never remove existing fields
First migration (v1_to_v2): no-op placeholder — v1-to-v2 handled by lazy defaults + write-back-on-touch
Registry is initialized on first Lithos startup if absent
Unit test: fresh registry creation
Unit test: idempotent migration application
make check passes

userStories.title: Differentiated note_type_priors
userStories.description: As the system, I want to tune note_type_priors based on MVP1 learning data patterns, so that different note types have retrieval weights reflecting their empirical utility.
acceptanceCriteria: Default note_type_priors in LcmaConfig updated to: observation=0.5, agent_finding=0.6, summary=0.55, concept=0.45, task_record=0.35, hypothesis=0.5
Existing configs that override note_type_priors continue to work — validator fills missing keys with new defaults
Priors remain configurable via LcmaConfig.note_type_priors — new defaults are not hardcoded in reranking logic
Unit test: verify new default values
Integration test: verify reranking score differs between note types
make check passes
